title: Solar-Farm Inverter Design Sweep (biased-DTLZ hypervolume)
strategies: trivial: prototype the single midpoint design x=[0.5]*n budget-many times -> one cost point, dominates baseline volume only -> ~0.1

greedy: uniform grid over the position variables in DECISION space, distance vars at 0.5 -> lands on the frontier but the (x**alpha) bias warps it into a clustered, uneven spread -> beats baseline but leaves volume on the table

strong: INVERT the bias (x_j = target**(1/alpha)) for an angle-even seed, then coordinate-wise local hypervolume ascent (multi-scale steps, keep only volume-increasing moves) -> reference-tuned spread on the warped frontier, materially more volume, still short of the global optimum
